title: Conjecture 2 (Jaeger, Linial, Payan, and Tarsi)
statement_text: For every prime number $p$, there is a constant $c(p)$ such that for any $t > c(p)$ linear bases $B_1, \ldots, B_t$ of $\mathbb{Z}_p^n$, the union $\bigcup_{s=1}^{t} B_s$ forms an additive basis of $\mathbb{Z}_p^n$.
context_text: Jaeger, Linial, Payan and Tarsi posed this in 1992 as a generalisation of the Cauchy-Davenport theorem to higher dimensions, and noted its connection to nowhere-zero flows. Alon, Linial and Meshulam had previously proved a weaker version whose bound on the number of bases depends on $n$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in the special case where the support of every vector in each basis has size at most two (Theorem 3), with an explicit bound $t > 8\ell(3p-4)+p-2$ depending on the number $\ell$ of distinct shadows of size 2.
paper_title: Additive bases and flows in graphs

title: Antisymmetric variant of Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture
statement_text: There is a constant $k$ such that every directed $k$-edge-connected graph has a $\mathbb{Z}_5$-antisymmetric flow.
context_text: The authors present this as the natural antisymmetric analogue of Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture (which was solved by Thomassen and later improved to 6-edge-connectivity by Lovász et al.). The note $\mathbb{Z}_5$ is best possible since $\mathbb{Z}_2$, $\mathbb{Z}_3$, and $\mathbb{Z}_4$ cannot support antisymmetric flows in general.
notes: Stated in prose as 'The natural antisymmetric variant … would be the following' without a labelled environment.
paper_title: Additive bases and flows in graphs

title: Conjecture 14
context_text: Corollary 13(iv) shows that directed 12-edge-connected graphs have a $\mathbb{Z}_5$-antisymmetric flow. On the other hand, a result of Nešetřil, Raspaud and Sopena implies (by duality) that there exist directed 7-edge-connected graphs with no $\mathbb{Z}_5$-asf, so 8-edge-connectivity is the first open case.
paper_title: Additive bases and flows in graphs
